skill.name: brevo-automation
skill.description: Automate Brevo email marketing workflows through Rube MCP, including campaign review, template management, sender checks, and A/B test configuration.
skill.summary: Manage Brevo campaigns, templates, senders, and A/B test settings through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, parameter names, and email fields in SKILL.md. The real risk is authorized network automation through Rube MCP that can modify Brevo campaigns, templates, senders, and A/B test settings, so publication is acceptable with a medium warning.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Brevo Account Changes Through External MCP
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to connect Rube MCP and run Brevo tools that can update campaigns, create or update templates, delete inactive templates, and configure A/B tests. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can change production marketing assets if used with a privileged Brevo connection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly require an external Rube MCP connection and list Brevo tools that modify campaigns and templates. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static Ruby shell backtick alerts refer to Markdown inline code for MCP tool names, parameters, and example snippets. SKILL.md contains instructions and reference text, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation using backticks to format tool names and placeholders. No executable script, Ruby runtime, or command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic operations. The cited lines describe the skill, sort order parameters, and email workflow fields.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and parameter descriptions, with no hash function, cipher, password handling, or security-sensitive cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alert points to a reply-to email address parameter for Brevo templates. It does not describe host discovery, port scanning, or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a normal email template field in the Brevo workflow. There is no scanning target, socket use, or discovery behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Rube MCP Endpoint Is Expected Configuration
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes the Rube MCP endpoint used to connect the required external service. This is a true hardcoded URL, but it is disclosed setup information for the declared dependency.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is directly visible and used for setup. It points to the declared Rube MCP service rather than an unexplained exfiltration endpoint.

content.value_statement: Brevo users often need to inspect campaigns, update templates, and verify sender details across several screens. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for doing those tasks through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Lists Brevo email campaigns with status, type, date, pagination, and statistics filters.
Updates campaign fields such as subject, sender, recipients, content, schedule, and A/B test settings.
Lists, creates, updates, and deletes Brevo email templates through the documented Rube MCP tools.
Retrieves verified Brevo senders for use in campaign and template configuration.
Explains required parameters, mutually exclusive fields, date formats, and common Brevo response shapes.
Guides users to search Rube MCP tools first so current schemas are used.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and an authenticated Brevo account.
Does not verify sender identities; verification must be completed in the Brevo web interface.
Does not send campaigns directly through a documented send tool in this skill.
Can affect real Brevo assets, so users should confirm campaign IDs and recipient lists before changes.

use_cases.title: Review Campaign Inventory
use_cases.description: List draft, queued, sent, or archived campaigns with pagination and optional statistics before planning updates.

use_cases.title: Maintain Reusable Templates
use_cases.description: Create or update Brevo templates with verified senders, personalization syntax, and active status checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare A/B Test Settings
use_cases.description: Find a classic campaign and configure subject variants, split rules, winner criteria, and winner delay.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Campaigns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Brevo automation skill to list my recent sent campaigns. Search Rube tools first, then show campaign names, IDs, status, and available statistics.

prompt_templates.title: Check Sender Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Brevo automation skill to list verified senders. Tell me which sender IDs can be used for a campaign update.

prompt_templates.title: Update A Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Brevo automation skill to find an inactive email template by name and prepare an update for its subject, sender, and HTML content.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Campaign Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Brevo automation skill to find a classic draft campaign and configure an A/B subject test with a 50 percent split, click winner criteria, and a 24 hour winner delay.

output_examples.input: List all draft campaigns and exclude HTML content from the response.
output_examples.output: A concise campaign table with campaign IDs, names, statuses, sender details, and pagination notes. The response omits full HTML bodies to reduce noise.

output_examples.input: Create a welcome email template for a verified sender.
output_examples.output: A preflight checklist confirms sender availability, required template name, subject, content length, active status, and personalization placeholders.

output_examples.input: Set up an A/B subject test on a campaign.
output_examples.output: A campaign update plan identifies the campaign ID, enables A/B testing, sets both subject variants, defines the split, and names the winner rule.

best_practices: Search Rube MCP tools before each workflow to confirm current Brevo schemas.
Use excludeHtmlContent when listing campaigns unless full email bodies are needed.
Confirm sender verification, recipient lists, and campaign IDs before any update.
anti_patterns: Do not update production campaigns without confirming the target campaign ID.
Do not pass both htmlContent and htmlUrl in the same campaign or template update.
Do not delete templates unless they are inactive and no longer used by active workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Brevo API key?
faq.answer: The skill expects Brevo access through Rube MCP. Users authenticate the Brevo connection through the Rube connection workflow.

faq.question: Can it send an email campaign?
faq.answer: The skill documents listing and updating campaigns. It notes that sending or scheduling may require the Brevo UI or available Rube tools.

faq.question: Can it create new email templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes the Brevo create or update template tool and the required fields for template creation.

faq.question: Can it verify a sender email address?
faq.answer: No. It can list verified senders, but sender verification is completed in the Brevo web interface.

faq.question: Why should tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use current parameters.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The skill can guide changes to real Brevo assets through an authenticated connection. Users should review targets before approving actions.
